prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `42f14c03`.
codex_response:
Rule: crop the unique non-background object whose bounding-box gaps split into slot shapes that also appear elsewhere as standalone components, then recolor each gap with the color of its matching external component.

I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint as the starting point and discarded the Sonnet summary because it described rectangle overlay instead of slot-shape matching; that correction is noted in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_42f14c03/notes.md#L1). No new shared primitive was needed.

The implementation is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_42f14c03/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_42f14c03/verifier.py#L6), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_42f14c03/generator.py#L145). The generator explicitly builds a 16x16 scene with one carved target object, matching external slot pieces, and carved/solid distractors, which matches the official family well on visual inspection.

Validation passed end to end: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I checked the preview sheet against the originals. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/42f14c03/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/42f14c03/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/42f14c03/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/42f14c03/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/42f14c03/previews/preview_03.png).
